product_updates.title: OpenClaw is finally available on Android and iOS
product_updates.content: The free open source agentic program is finally invading your phone.

product_updates.title: The DeepMind trio who built a poker AI are now making money for quant hedge funds
product_updates.content: EquiLibre Technologies, a Prague-based AI lab founded by three ex-DeepMind researchers, is now valued at more than $500 million.

product_updates.title: Google introduces a faster, cheaper image generator with Nano Banana 2 Lite
product_updates.content: Google is updating its image generator to make it faster and cheaper, making it a more useful tool for creators looking to make AI content.

product_updates.title: Nvidia competitor Etched hits $5B valuation, $1B in sales for AI chip
product_updates.content: Nvidia AI chip competitor Etched says it has already booked $1 billion under contract for the inference systems powered by its chip.

product_updates.title: Anthropic launches Claude Sonnet 5 as a cheaper way to run agents
product_updates.content: Anthropic’s Claude Sonnet 5 brings stronger agentic capabilities, lower pricing, and improved safety, positioning the model as a cheaper alternative to Opus, GPT-5.5, and Gemini Pro.

product_updates.title: Acti puts AI agents directly into your smartphone keyboard
product_updates.content: Acti is betting the smartphone keyboard is the next home for AI assistants. The startup's new keyboard for iOS and Android works across apps and lets users create custom AI-powered shortcuts using natural language.

product_updates.title: Anthropic’s Claude Science bets on workflow, not a new model, to win over scientists
product_updates.content: Anthropic's Claude Science is a workbench that gives scientists one environment to do computational research, saving them from the need to bounce between databases, pipelines, and tools.

product_updates.title: X now offers an MCP server to make its platform easier for AI tools to use
product_updates.content: X has launched a hosted MCP server, making it easier for developers to connect AI applications with the company’s API.

product_updates.title: Podcasting platform Riverside enters the newsletter publishing game
product_updates.content: Users will be able use AI to create newsletters based on their recordings.

product_updates.title: Amazon launches new $1 billion FDE org, following OpenAI and Anthropic
product_updates.content: Engineers on the new team will embed within companies to deploy purpose-built agents, focusing on fast deployments and customer self-sufficiency.

technical_breakthroughs.title: Claude Science is Anthropic’s newest flagship product
technical_breakthroughs.content: At an event for pharmaceutical executives, biotech founders, and researchers on Tuesday, Anthropic announced Claude Science, a major new product intended to support scientific research in the same way that Claude Code supports software engineering. Like Claude Code, Claude Science can autonomousl…

technical_breakthroughs.title: New attack provides one more reason why AI browsers are a bad idea
technical_breakthroughs.content: Telling an LLM that 2 + 2 = 5 is enough to make it follow forbidden instructions.

technical_breakthroughs.title: Roundtables: Longevity’s Next Frontier: “Reprogramming” Your Body
technical_breakthroughs.content: Listen to the session or watch below Billions of dollars are flooding into efforts to reverse aging as scientists explore ways to return cells to a younger state. But how far off are these experimental treatments? Will they really work? Watch a conversation exploring longevity’s new focus. Speake…

The Download: AI “coworkers” and stratospheric internet

technical_breakthroughs.title: Agriculture is ready for AI, but its data isn’t
technical_breakthroughs.content: Artificial intelligence is transforming what is possible in agriculture, but industry leaders should be wary of investing in AI without first laying the groundwork. The use cases are promising, especially for an industry navigating volatile fertilizer costs, unpredictable weather, and margins tha…

technical_breakthroughs.title: Building tech in the world’s secret R&D hub
technical_breakthroughs.content: Apple. Anthropic. Disney Research. Google. Meta. Microsoft. NVIDIA. OpenAI. Few places outside Silicon Valley can claim R&D hubs from all of these companies. Fewer still are concentrated in a city of just over 400,000 people—roughly half the size of San Francisco. Over the past two decades, howev…
